functions: permit, deny, refuse, quarantine, or fail closed before consequence
preserve stable event and transition identifiers
emit replayable JSON or JSONL records
separate display, custody, admissibility, execution, and publication authority
dependencies: compatible handoff or canonical event input
authorized execution provider when execution is requested
custody and reconstruction destinations when authoritative retention is required
limitations: public Site is a projection and not custody or execution authority
framework comparison does not itself establish admissibility, certification, or superiority
reciprocal live testing has not yet occurred
exclusions: does not claim ownership of every upstream or downstream architecture
does not infer parentage from scope overlap
does not treat publication as execution authority
